mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Excessively high inflation (CPIAUCSL > 2.5% monthly) triggers central bank rate hikes, reducing equity valuations and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5%, or inflation-adjusted earnings growth outperforms bond yields.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous improvement in payroll growth (PAYEMS > 100k) and declining unemployment (UNRATE < 7%) signals strong labor market dynamics that drive corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative in > 40% of periods where both PAYEMS exceeds 100k and UNRATE falls below 7%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising residential construction (HOUST > 900k) combined with expanding GDP (GDPC1 > 2% q/q) indicates strong economic demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative in > 35% of periods where both HOUST exceeds 900k and GDP growth exceeds 2%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in industrial production (INDPRO > 1.5% m/m) signals robust corporate activity, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative in > 50% of instances where INDPRO growth exceeds 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong consumer spending (RSAFS > 420k) combined with low interest rates (FEDFUNDS < 1%) creates favorable conditions for equity markets, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative in > 40% of periods where both RSAFS exceeds 420k and FEDFUNDS remains below 1%.

mechanism_narrative: Monetary tightening cycles (FEDFUNDS rising > 0.5% in 3 months) combined with elevated inflation (CPIAUCSL > 2%) lead to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 0.5% increase in 3 months AND CPIAUCSL > 2%
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in > 50% of instances where both FEDFUNDS rises 0.5% and CPIAUCSL exceeds 2%.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent job creation (PAYEMS > 150k) and declining unemployment (UNRATE < 7.5%) indicate a strengthening labor market, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative in > 45% of periods with PAYEMS above 150k and UNRATE below 7.5%.
